title: CUDA-built torchvision eager _meta_registrations import crashes on NPU
problem_description: When torchvision is installed as a CUDA build (e.g., torchvision==0.20.1+cu124 via pip resolving against torch==2.5.1 on a system with CUDA libs present), its __init__.py eagerly imports _meta_registrations. This module calls @torch.library.register_fake for CUDA C++ custom ops (nms, roi_align, deform_conv2d) that do not exist in the NPU PyTorch dispatch table, causing 'RuntimeError: operator torchvision::nms does not exist' at import time — even when the project never uses these ops.
rough_fix_approach: Wrap the `from torchvision import _meta_registrations` statement in torchvision/__init__.py (typically lines 10-13) with a try/except RuntimeError guard. Confirm the project only needs pure-Python torchvision symbols (e.g., InterpolationMode enum from torchvision.transforms) and does not depend on the C++ ops. If ops are needed, install torchvision from source against torch-npu instead.
artifact_evidence: execution_journal.jsonl:line 11 (Phase 5 total duration 8367s reflects iterative repair)

involved_code_files.role: Third-party dependency — import of _meta_registrations at line ~11 triggers @torch.library.register_fake for CUDA-only C++ ops
Entry script — imports transformers which transitively imports torchvision via torchvision.transforms.InterpolationMode

reasoning: This is a systemic venv-level issue: pip frequently resolves torchvision to a CUDA build when torch (non-cpu) is in requirements.txt alongside torch-npu, because the default PyTorch index serves CUDA builds. The _meta_registrations eager import pattern exists across many torchvision versions (0.15+). Any project importing transformers or torchvision on an NPU venv where torchvision resolved to +cu124 will hit this. The fix is verified NPU-native with no CPU fallback (model loads 7 shards on Ascend 910B2C). Not covered by Phase 4 rules (not a code-level .cuda() → .npu() replacement).
